text:
Dan Evans' dream of earning an Olympic gold medal for himself andAndy Murrayremains alive after the pair's remarkable comeback in the first round of the men's doubles. The British duo saved five match points against Japan's Kei Nishikori and Taro Daniel in Sunday's (July 28) epic match-up they eventually won 2-6, 7-6 (5), 11-9.

The pair were 4-9 down in the third set and on the verge of eliminationin what would have been Murray's last game of his career but their determination was unrelenting as they managed to escape defeat and overcome the Japanese duo where they'll take on the winner of France’s Arthur Fils and Ugo Humbert or Sander Gillé and Joran Vliegen of Belgium on Tuesday (July 30).

Evans and Murray enjoyed jubilant celebrations after their triumph with the former keeping his dream alive of winning a gold medal for himself and his team-mate who was forced to withdraw from the Wimbledon mixed doubles tournament earlier this month afterEmma Raducanupulled out due to stiffness in her wrist.

Our community members are treated to special offers, promotions, and adverts from us and our partners. You can check out at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

The withdrawal of Raducanu meant that the Olympics would be the last competition of Murray's career. Evans showed what a team player he is by insisting he desperately wants to pay the ultimate tribute to Britain's greatest-ever tennis player by winning a medal. "There's nothing more that I want than to get a medal for him and myself," the 34-year-old said before their first-round clash.

Murray has won two gold medals in men's singles in 2012 and 2016 and according to Evans, he's "competitive as ever" in his fifth and final Olympics. "It's really positive that we agreed on that and we can work together on giving ourselves the best chance," he said.

"I don't see any reason why we can't do something special. I don't think Andy wants it to be a farewell, I think he wants to win a medal and he'll say goodbye in his own way. He's here to do a job and get a medal. He was out there the other day doing sprints at the end of our session. Knowing Andy as I do, he's as competitive as ever, working as hard as ever.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

"It’s unbelievable that he has done it in doubles. It pretty much sums him up that this is his last tournament and he has [a ­comeback]. I’m always in the changing room ­saying: ‘Ah, another long one for him,’ and now I’m on the court with him. It was something I’ll remember for ever, but it’d be amazing if we went on to do something really special this week after that."

The 37-year-old has conjured up many a comeback in his career but Sunday's one is among the best of his career he revealed. "It’s probably up there," he said. "Pro­bably the way that we were playing to that stage would have not suggested that we were likely to come back. I certainly didn’t feel like I was playing well. Evo played well at times but for both of us we can play much better than that. And then it just all clicked at the end and we played some pretty, pretty good stuff."

title: Dan Evans shows true colours with Andy Murray comments Raducanu snub | Tennis | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fasika Zelealem
description: Dan Evans' hope of delivering himself and Andy Murray a gold medal at the Olympics continues after their epic comeback in the men's doubles.
